mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ rate decline observations, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE changes are below 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the industrial production index during inflationary periods reflects supply bottlenecks that compress corporate margins and force negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods yield positive 20-day returns in > 50% of observations, or the mechanism reverses direction when CPIAUCSL growth falls below 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between the federal funds rate and consumer price inflation indicates real rate compression that stimulates short-term equity buying pressure within one week.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL spread expansion fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or the signal breaks down when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate weakening economic activity that systematically reduces corporate earnings expectations and generates negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the rolling 5-year out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue expansion and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5% is followed by negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for prior quarter momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising unemployment during liquidity crunches triggers rapid risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 5-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) during periods of VIX > 25, or the signal becomes positive during stress episodes.
